compatibility_note.message: The older Program Files installation reported: The queries were authored with a newer version of Power BI Desktop and might not work with your version.
compatibility_note.resolution: Reopened and QAed the exact output/dashboard_final.pbix in the newer Store Power BI Desktop 2.155.756.0.

project.path: Project 19 - Finance Data Quality BI Governance
project.final_pbix: Project 19 - Finance Data Quality BI Governance/output/dashboard_final.pbix

root_cause.primary: KPI sparkline SVG data was built from daily-grain DimDate rows. Multiple dates shared the same MonthIndex, so many SVG points received the same X coordinate and collapsed into vertical strokes.
root_cause.secondary: The prior RANKX-style point spacing did not guarantee one point per month in the image-card DAX.
root_cause.not_broken: The dashboard data itself was not static; the sparkline axis grain and point-spacing logic were wrong.

fixes_applied: Kept KPI cards as native image visuals.
Built AxisMonths as one row per MonthIndex using SELECTCOLUMNS, DISTINCT, selected-month logic, and trailing-12 fallback.
Calculated each sparkline value over the full month context with CALCULATE over ALL(DimDate) filtered by MonthIndex.
Computed X coordinates from MonthIndex range rather than daily rows or visual row rank.
Preserved the clean sparkline treatment: no decorative grey bars, no wedge area fill, no vertical stems, thin line, subtle reference line, tiny start marker, and stronger end marker.

Rebuilt PBIP with build_project19.py, opened PBIP in Power BI Desktop, refreshed data after the incomplete/no-data prompt, Save As to output/dashboard_final.pbix, then closed and reopened the exact PBIX.

final_pbix_file_evidence.path: Project 19 - Finance Data Quality BI Governance/output/dashboard_final.pbix

counts.count_source: PBIP report definition rebuilt by build_project19.py and then opened, refreshed, saved, closed, and reopened as the exact final PBIX in Power BI Desktop.

slicer_interaction_tests.sparkline_result: pass - KPI lines redraw left-to-right instead of vertical collapse
pass - trend shape changes from Revenue
pass - zero incident context renders without image placeholder or vertical stem collapse
pass - KPI cards visibly change and risk coloring updates
pass - trend uses monthly spacing and no grey scrollbar/chrome appears

mandatory_qa_passes.name: final PBIX opened in Power BI Desktop
mandatory_qa_passes.evidence: The exact output/dashboard_final.pbix opened in Microsoft Store Power BI Desktop 2.155.756.0 and rendered Governance Overview.

mandatory_qa_passes.name: close and reopen exact final PBIX
mandatory_qa_passes.evidence: Closed Power BI Desktop, reopened the exact output/dashboard_final.pbix, verified window title dashboard_final and saved timestamp Today at 2:15 AM.

mandatory_qa_passes.name: KPI row after reopen
mandatory_qa_passes.evidence: KPI row screenshot after reopen shows left-to-right 12-month microtrends for DQ Score, Freshness SLA, Refresh Success, and Open Incidents.

mandatory_qa_passes.name: all 4 pages after reopen
mandatory_qa_passes.evidence: Captured reopened Desktop screenshots for Governance Overview, Reliability & Quality, Adoption & Controls, and Risk & Action Queue.

mandatory_qa_passes.name: slicer interaction scan
mandatory_qa_passes.evidence: Five Domain slicer combinations visibly changed KPI values and sparkline shapes.

mandatory_qa_passes.name: Escape/unselected visual chrome scan
mandatory_qa_passes.evidence: After Escape/unselected checks, KPI cards did not expose unwanted KPI scrollbars, white headers, image placeholders, or visual chrome.

05-kpi-card-composition.notes: KPI structure remains title, current value, PY, YoY, and microtrend; PY/YoY are readable.

06-sparkline-layout-collision.notes: Sparkline now uses monthly grain and MonthIndex spacing; no vertical strokes, collision, scrollbar, or placeholder returned.

09-chart-card-visual-polish.notes: Sparkline reads as finance microtrend rather than bullet chart or collapsed axis artifact.

11-visual-chrome-and-artifact-cleanup.notes: KPI cards have no unwanted scrollbar/chrome in reopened Desktop screenshots; only legitimate long table visuals retain scrollbars.

remaining_caveats: The final PBIX requires the newer Microsoft Store Power BI Desktop 2.155.756.0 or later. The older Program Files installation 2.154.1260.0 cannot open it and shows a newer-version query warning.
Desktop authoring panes are visible in screenshots because QA was performed in Power BI Desktop as required.
Slicer test screenshots intentionally show the dropdown open so the selected Domain value is visible; baseline reopened screenshots show the report canvas without KPI scrollbars/chrome.
Only legitimate long detail tables retain internal scrollbars.
